narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A fleet behind an Application Load Balancer using round-robin distribution. Cluster-level aggregates show a wide p50-to-p95 spread because some instances are persistently overloaded while others are persistently idle. The fix is a load-balancing algorithm change, not a capacity change.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests the Compute Analyst's ability to read per-instance variance from the per_instance_breakdown fixture and recognize that the cluster-level average masks a distribution problem. Capacity adjustments would not fix the imbalance.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Reconfigure the target group from round_robin to least_outstanding_requests. Expected: per-instance CPU variance drops below 12%.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation identifies the imbalance. A great recommendation specifies the algorithm change explicitly (round_robin → least_outstanding_requests) and notes that capacity adjustments would not address the root cause.

before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: CPU variance across instances dropped below 12%, application_p95_latency_ms converged to the 200–240ms band across all instances, SLA 99.9% preserved. No capacity change required.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across api-gateway cohort during Q4 2025.
